note:
情报官员长期以来一直警告称，俄罗斯特工将不可避免地试图干预2020年的竞选活动-现在，一些人似乎已经被抓获，目标是近200个政治行动，其中包括一家与拜登竞选活动关系深厚的关键通讯公司。
路透社(Reuters)周四上午报道，疑似俄罗斯政府支持的黑客试图侵入总部位于华盛顿的SKDKickerbocker的系统，SKDKickerbocker是一家战略和传播公司，与乔·拜登(Joe Biden)的竞选活动密切合作。过去两个月发生的袭击没有成功。
这一失败的黑客企图是由微软引起SKDK注意的，据报道，微软收集了与克里姆林宫有关联的黑客的信息，确定与克里姆林宫有关联的黑客是最有可能的嫌疑人。据称，这些攻击主要集中在网络钓鱼上，这是一种常见的黑客攻击方式，诱使用户泄露敏感密码。
这是俄罗斯黑客在2016年总统大选之前访问民主党全国委员会电子邮件的方法，这些电子邮件随后在网上泄露。
一位熟悉SKDK击退黑客企图的人士表示，这些特工并没有走得太远，他告诉路透社：“他们戒备森严，所以没有入侵。”另一位消息人士称，无法确认拜登的竞选活动是否是目标，或者俄罗斯人是否试图收集SKDK其他客户长长名单上的情报。
SKDK与民主党的关系极为密切-该公司董事总经理安妮塔·邓恩(Anita Dunn)曾在巴拉克·奥巴马(Barack Obama)担任总统期间担任白宫公关总监，现在拜登竞选团队担任高级顾问。
这并不是微软第一次成功挫败疑似俄罗斯政府黑客-2017年，这家软件制造商通过起诉臭名昭著的黑客组织花花熊(Fancy Bear)，确定了克里姆林宫网络间谍活动的120多个新目标。微软的法律行动导致70台命令和控制服务器被查封，黑客用这些服务器在受害者电脑上秘密引导恶意软件。
微软还帮助挫败了俄罗斯情报人员，这些情报人员在参议员克莱尔·麦卡斯基尔(Claire McCaskill)开始2018年连任竞选时，通过网络钓鱼活动袭击了她。
不出所料，克里姆林宫很快否认与最近的网络钓鱼企图有任何联系。在俄罗斯政府所有的塔斯社(TASS)报道的评论中，弗拉基米尔·普京(Vladimir Putin)总统的新闻秘书德米特里·佩斯科夫(Dmitry Peskov)表示，他不知道SKDK是什么，并补充说，“不幸的是，它看起来像是由有时受人尊敬的机构发布的另一种胡说八道。”
美国情报官员早在去年2月就向众议院议员通报称，俄罗斯演员已经在干预2020年的选举，这再次有利于特朗普。这场简报会是由时任代理国家情报总监约瑟夫·马奎尔(Joseph Maguire)的一名助手为众议院情报委员会举行的，马奎尔后来被特朗普赶下了台。
本月早些时候，情报官员公开证实了他们的看法，即俄罗斯正在使用一系列技术来损害拜登。上周，联邦调查局(FBI)介入警告Facebook，俄罗斯的巨魔农场正在以非营利性新闻机构的身份游行，试图推动左翼对拜登的批评。
国土安全部还发现了俄罗斯的一个阴谋，散布有关拜登心理健康的虚假信息-这是特朗普攻击他的对手的一个反复出现的主题。
拜登上周五表示，特朗普政府关于中国是美国选举最大威胁的说法是错误的，他说：“我认为世界上有很多国家会乐于看到我们的选举不稳定。但最努力、最始终如一、从未松懈的是俄罗斯。“。
周四晚些时候，微软宣布，观察到俄罗斯黑客在过去“几个月”攻击了至少200个组织，包括民主党和共和党政治顾问、州和国家层面的政党、美国智库和欧洲政党。该公司表示，其软件屏蔽了“大多数”企图的黑客，并已通知所有目标或被攻破的人。
微软补充说，与中国政府有关联的黑客组织试图侵入与拜登竞选有关的网络、知名国际关系专家、智库，以及“至少一名曾与特朗普政府有关联的知名人士”。
据微软称，一个伊朗黑客组织在5月和6月也曾试图侵入特朗普竞选相关账户，但均以失败告终。
note_en:
Intelligence officials have  long been warning that Russian agents will inevitably  try to interfere in the 2020 campaign—now some appear to have been caught targeting nearly 200 political operations, including a a key communications firm with deep ties to the Biden campaign.
 Reuters reported Thursday morning that suspected  Russian state-backed hackers have attempted to breach the systems at Washington-based SKDKnickerbocker, a strategy and communications firm working hand-in-glove with Joe Biden’s campaign. The attacks, which took place over the past two months, were unsuccessful.
 The failed hacking attempt was brought to SKDK’s attention by Microsoft, which reportedly gathered information identifying hackers linked to the Kremlin as the most likely suspects. The attacks are said to have mainly focused on phishing—a common hacking method which lures users into disclosing sensitive passwords.
 That was  the method used by Russian hackers to access DNC emails, which were subsequently leaked online, ahead of the 2016 presidential election.
  A person familiar with SKDK’s repellingof the hacking attempts said the agents didn’t get very far, telling Reuters: “They are well-defended, so there has been no breach.” Another source said it was impossible to confirm if Biden’s campaign was the target, or whether the Russians were trying to gather intel on the long list of other SKDK clients.
 SKDK has an extremely close relationship with the Democratic Party—the firm’s managing director, Anita Dunn, served as a White House communications director during the Barack Obama presidency and now  works for the Biden campaign as a top adviser.
  It’s not the first time Microsoft has managed to thwart suspected Russian government hackers—in 2017, the software maker  identified over 120 new targets of the Kremlin’s cyber spying by suing the notorious hacker group known as Fancy Bear. Microsoft’s legal action led to the seizure of 70 command-and-control servers the hackers used to covertly direct malware on victim computers.
 Microsoft also helped to foil the Russian intelligence agents who  targeted Sen. Claire McCaskill with a phishing campaign as she began her 2018 re-election campaign.
 Unsurprisingly, the Kremlin was quick to deny any connection to the latest attempted phishing attempts. In comments reported by the Russian government-owned TASS news agency, President Vladimir Putin’s press secretary, Dmitry Peskov, said he had no idea what SKDK is and added, ”It looks like just another nonsense that is published, unfortunately, by a sometimes respected agency.”
  U.S. intelligence officials briefed House lawmakers  as early as last February that Russian actors were already interfering in the 2020 elections, once more to the benefit of Trump. The briefing was conducted for the House Intelligence Committee by an aide to then acting Director of National Intelligence Joseph Maguire, who was  later forced out of his position by Trump.
 Earlier this month, intelligence officials  publicly confirmed their belief that Russia is using a range of techniques to damage Biden and, last week, the FBI  stepped in to warn Facebook that Russia’s troll farm was parading as a nonprofit news organization in an attempt to promote criticism of Biden from the left.
 The Department of Homeland Security has  also discovered a Russian plot spreading disinformation about Biden’s mental health—a reoccurring theme of Trump’s attacks on his rival.
 Biden  said last Friday that the Trump administration is wrong about China being the biggest threat to U.S. elections, saying, “There are a lot of countries around the world I think would be happy to see our elections destabilized. But the one who’s working the hardest most consistently and never has let up is Russia.”
 Later on Thursday, Microsoft  announced that it had observed Russian hackers target at least 200 organizations, including Democratic and Republican political consultants, political parties at the state and national level, American think tanks, and European political parties over the past “several months.” The company said that its software blocked “most” of the attempted hacks and that it has notified all of those targeted or breached.
 Hacking groups linked to the Chinese government attempt to break into networks associated with the Biden campaign, prominent international relations pundits, think tanks, and “at least one prominent individual formerly associated with the Trump Administration,” Microsoft added.
 An Iranian hacking group also tried and failed to break into Trump campaign-related accounts in May and June, according to Microsoft.

note:
根据美国版权法，有很多事情是不允许人们做的，但也许同样重要的是，也有豁免。
美国版权局定期审查DMCA第1201条的这些豁免，该条款防止公众“修修补补”受DRM保护的内容和设备。
这些规定在办事处听取利益攸关方和公众的各种论点后每三年更新一次。这也允许感兴趣的各方提出新的豁免建议。
在2018年的上一次更新中，怀旧游戏玩家取得了一场虽小但意义重大的胜利。为了给子孙后代保留“废弃的”游戏，版权局将游戏保护豁免扩大到需要在线组件的游戏。
这是一个至关重要的补充，因为现在大多数游戏都有在线方面。有了新的豁免，合法拥有视频游戏服务器代码和游戏本地代码副本的保存机构被允许打破DRM和其他技术限制，使这些游戏可以玩。
这种“修修补补”的做法，现在被政府视为合理使用，政府拒绝了各大游戏公司的批评，他们担心图书馆和博物馆可能会利用这项权利作商业用途，从而影响它们的销售。
几周前，版权局开始了对DMCA豁免的最新审查，该审查将于明年更新。从那时起，来自档案工作者、数字权利和消费者组织的几份意见书陆续收到。其中几项要求该办公室延长目前对被遗弃的网络游戏的豁免。
软件保护网络(SPN)和图书馆版权联盟(LCA)指出，这一新的豁免确保了经典游戏将得到保护。这使得怀旧的游戏玩家和年轻一代可以玩不再得到官方支持的老游戏。这已经带来了一些成功的故事。
“例如，佐治亚理工学院图书馆的计算实验室，RetroTECH，有大量回收的视频游戏机，其中许多根据§1201豁免可供研究和教学使用。SPN和LCS写道：“数十款GameBoy Advance、游戏机和PC游戏现在可以得到保护，版权侵权索赔或法律诉讼的风险更低。”
延长豁免的呼吁得到了非营利性组织消费者报告(Consumer Reports)的支持，该组织指出，豁免“证明对消费者消除了保留他们享受的视频游戏功能的这一障碍非常有利。”
除了更新目前的规则外，SPN和LCS也要求扩大。目前，如果需要，他们可以打破DRM，但这些游戏只能在图书馆和博物馆等“合格”机构的场所内提供。
“SPN和LCA要求扩大电子游戏保护豁免[…]。以消除该计划不得在符合条件的机构的实际办公场所之外分发或提供的要求，“他们写道。
一如既往，目前的DMCA审查将需要几个月的时间才能完成。虽然这一要求肯定会得到考虑，但游戏公司可能会反对这一新建议，就像他们过去反复做的那样。
让版权局采纳这项豁免的功劳很大程度上要归功于旧金山的艺术与数字娱乐博物馆(The Made)，该博物馆在三年前提交了请愿书。
深受许多博彩迷喜爱的博物馆最近不得不关闭大门，将藏品存放在仓库里。然而，就像许多被遗弃的游戏一样，它不会永远消失。Made目前正在筹集资金，准备在其他地方重生。
note_en:
There are a lot of things people are not allowed to do under US copyright law, but perhaps just as importantly there are exemptions too.
 The U.S. Copyright Office regularly reviews these exemptions to Section 1201 of the DMCA, which prevents the public from ‘tinkering’ with DRM-protected content and devices.
 These provisions are renewed every three years after the Office hears various arguments from stakeholders and the general public. This also allows interested parties to suggest new exemptions.
 During the  last update in 2018, there was a  small but significant win for nostalgic gamers. To preserve ‘abandoned’ games for future generations, the Copyright Office expanded the game preservation exemptions to games that require an online component.
 This was a crucial addition, as most games nowadays have an online aspect. With the new exemption, preservation institutions that legally possess a copy of a video game’s server code and the game’s local code were allowed to break DRM and other technological restrictions to make these playable.
 This type of “tinkering” is now seen as fair use by the Government, which rejected critique from the major game companies who feared that libraries and museums might exploit this right for commercial purposes, which would  hurt their sales.
 A few weeks ago the Copyright Office started its  latest review of the DMCA exemptions which will be updated next year. Since then, several submissions from archivists, digital rights, and consumer organizations have come in. Several of these ask the Office to renew the current exemptions for abandoned online games.
 The Software Preservation Network (SPN) and the Library Copyright Alliance (LCA) note that this new exemption ensures that classic games will be preserved. This allows nostalgic gamers and younger generations to play older games that are no longer officially supported. This has already led to some success stories.
 “For instance, Georgia Tech Library’s Computing Lab, retroTECH, has a significant collection of recovered video game consoles, many of which are made accessible for research and teaching uses by the §1201 exemption. Dozens of Gameboy Advance, console and PC games can now be preserved, with lower risks of copyright infringement claims or legal action,” SPN and LCS write.
 The call to renew the exemption is supported by the nonprofit group Consumer Reports, which notes that the exemption “has proven very beneficial to consumers in removing this obstacle to preserving the functionality of video games they enjoy.”
 In addition to renewing the current rules, SPN and LCS have also requested an expansion. At the moment, they are allowed to break DRM, if needed, but these games can only be made available inside the premises of ‘eligible’ institutions such as libraries and museums.
  “SPN and the LCA request expansion of the video game preservation exemption […] to eliminate the requirement that the program not be distributed or made available outside of the physical premises of an eligible institution,”  they write.
 As always, the current DMCA review will take a few months to be completed. While the request will certainly be considered, it’s possible that games companies will object to the new suggestion, as they have done repeatedly in the past.
 Much of the credit for getting the Copyright Office to adopt the present exemption goes to San Francisco’s Museum of Art and Digital Entertainment ( The MADE), which filed its petition three years ago.
 The Museum, which is loved by many gaming fans, recently had to close its doors and put its collection into storage. However, like many abandoned games, it’s not gone forever. The MADE is  currently raising money to respawn elsewhere.
title: 档案保管员希望对“废弃的”在线游戏给予更广泛的DMCA豁免
title_en: Archivists Want Broader DMCA Exemption for 'Abandoned' Online Games

note:
在发布前的几个月里，摩托罗拉的Razr引起了极大的炒作--例如，我们的快速动手能力是我们制作过的所有无性机器人视频中点击量最多的。即使是功能上完美的折叠式也很难达到人们的期望，如果你错过了，我们肯定没有得到完美的折叠式。这使得摩托罗拉几乎别无选择，只能埋头苦干，做出一些改变，然后再试一次。
这就是新款Razr的用武之地--它采用了经过修改的5G设计，并至少解决了第一款手机臭名昭著的一些问题。请注意，它仍然不是一款旗舰手机，1400美元的价格，我们也不确定它是不是一款划算的手机。但对于那些想要一款非常便于口袋使用、在关闭时也可以使用的人来说，摩托罗拉可能是在正确的道路上。
摩托罗拉一直很清楚，Razr是一款设计至上的设备，它不遗余力地重现了其经典翻盖手机在推出第一款折叠式手机时所表现出的视觉氛围。不过，为了给这款新机型增添一些急需的额外功能，摩托罗拉不得不做了一些改变：新的Razr稍微丰满了一些，下巴也没有原来的Razr那么突出。就个人而言，这些改变足以让Razr在视觉上不那么引人注目，但当你考虑到摩托罗拉可以在这里装些什么时，这些改变是值得的。
首先，摩托罗拉在Razr&#39；的上半部分塞了一个更好的摄像头。我对第一代1600万像素的Razr‘s后置摄像头最大的不满并不是它本身不好--只是和你在同等价位的手机上能找到的其他摄像头相比，它并不是很棒。作为回应，摩托罗拉为这款新机型选择了4800万像素的摄像头，这将大大提高照片质量。(鉴于我们甚至还没碰过这个东西，我们得看看这件事了。)。
在第一款Razr中发现的有点狭隘的骁龙710也不见了，取而代之的是更现代的骁龙765G和8 GB的RAM。这就是你在新一批价格实惠、支持5G的智能手机中看到的相同的芯片组，比如一加诺德(OnePlus Nord)、TCL 10 5G，以及LG天鹅绒的某些版本。正如我所说的，我们不是与旗舰动力合作，但新的Razr有它需要的一切，以更顺利地运行这一次。
说到提速，新款Razr的设计初衷是与低于6G的5G网络很好地配合，比如它的美国运营商合作伙伴T-Mobile和AT&amp；T运营的那些网络。(那是对的。尽管Verizon推出了第一款Razr，但显然没有计划提供这款大幅升级的机型。)。当然，额外的马力和网络支持可能会影响功耗，因此摩托罗拉也给了新款Razr略大的电池。我的意思也是稍微大一点--它的满容量最高可达2800mAh，高于最初的2510mAh。我认为任何改进都是一件好事，但这也意味着Razr用户正在使用的电池仍然比市面上大多数其他手机的电池要小得多。
当然，摩托罗拉认为电池续航时间不应该成为问题。这在一定程度上是因为它了解了人们如何使用Razr--显然，消费者真的很喜欢使用这款手机2.7英寸的外置触摸屏，它比内部大屏幕耗电少得多。问题是，最初的Razr&#39；软件没有太多的灵活性；你可以用那个较小的屏幕来检查你的通知，给你的自拍装框，或者发出对一条消息的预录回复，但仅此而已。
摩托罗拉为第二代Razr所做的最大改变是取消了这些限制。在这个小屏幕上向右滑动，你就可以快速访问摩托罗拉为方便使用而精心设计的几个应用程序，比如YouTube、谷歌新闻、谷歌主页等等。更棒的是，你可以在快捷键列表中添加任何你想要的应用程序，所以你很有可能在一天中至少有一部分时间不用打开Razr。
到目前为止，这听起来像是摩托罗拉在所有方面都改进了这款新的Razr，这几乎是真的。摩托罗拉只有几样东西没有改变，比如6.2英寸的柔性内部显示屏。这和他们上次使用的面板一模一样，虽然这不一定是件坏事，但我仍然希望第二代Razr屏幕的分辨率能高于876x2142。
也许更奇怪的是，无论如何在美国，摩托罗拉只是计划将这款手机命名为Razr；Razr；，而不打算将其与今年早些时候发布的仅限Verizon发布的机型区分开来。在美国，摩托罗拉只是计划将这款手机命名为Razr；Razr；，而不打算将其与今年早些时候发布的Verizon专用机型区分开来。我想这在某种程度上是有道理的--走进AT&amp；T-Mobile专卖店的人们可能没有想到会买到他们在Verizon找到的同样的设备，但当你考虑到这两款手机有多么不同时，把这两款手机放在同一个品牌桶里似乎非常奇怪。
话又说回来，现在可折叠的景观整体上是相当奇怪的--摩托罗拉(Motorola)有很好的伙伴。如果该公司对Razr的精致方法正在向你发出召唤，你不需要等待很长时间：它将于今年秋天在百思买(Best Buy)、百安居(B&amp；H Photo)、亚马逊(Amazon.com)和摩托罗拉(Motorola.com)以及美国电话电报公司(AT&amp；T)和T-Mobile同时解锁。
note_en:
In the months before its launch, Motorola's Razr generated ungodly levels of hype -- our  quick hands-on, for instance, has the most views of any non-sex robot video we've ever made. Even a functionally perfect foldable would've had a hard time living up to expectations, and in case you missed it, we most certainly  did not get a perfect foldable. That left Motorola will little choice but to buckle down, make some changes, and try again.
 That's where the brand's new Razr comes in -- it sports a modified design, 5G, and fixes for at least some of the issues the first model was notorious for. Mind you, it's still not a flagship phone, and at  $1400 we're not sure it's a great deal either. But for people who want an extremely pocket-friendly foldable that's also usable while closed, Motorola just might be on the right track.
  Motorola was always clear that the Razr is a "design-first" device, and it went to great lengths to recreate the visual vibe that its classic flip phones ran with for its first foldable. To pack some much-needed extras into this new model, though, Motorola had to make some changes: The new Razr is a little chubbier, and a features a "chin" that's a bit less prominent than the original's. Personally, these changes are enough to make the Razr just a little less visually striking, but they're worth it when you consider what Motorola could pack in here as a result.
  For one, Motorola squeezed a better camera into the Razr's top half. My biggest gripe with the original Razr's 16-megapixel rear shooter wasn't that it was bad, per se -- it just wasn't great compared to every other camera you'd find in a similarly priced phone. In response, Motorola chose a 48-megapixel camera for this new model, which  should improve photo quality substantially. (Seeing as we haven't even touched this thing yet, we'll have to see about that.)
 The somewhat pokey Snapdragon 710 found in the first Razr also is gone, replaced here by a more modern Snapdragon 765G and 8GB of RAM. That's the same great-but-not-quite-premium chipset you'll see in a new batch of affordable, 5G-friendly smartphones, like the  OnePlus Nord, the  TCL 10 5G, and  certain versions of the LG Velvet. As I said, we're not working with flagship power here, but the new Razr has everything it needs to run much more smoothly this time around.
 And speaking of speed boosts, the new Razr was built to play nice with sub-6 5G networks, like those operated by its US carrier partners, T-Mobile and AT&T. (That's right. Despite Verizon carrying the first Razr, there are apparently no plans for it to offer this significantly upgraded model.) Naturally, that extra horsepower and networking support are likely to impact power consumption, so Motorola also gave the new Razr a slightly bigger battery. I do mean  slightly bigger, too -- its full capacity tops out at 2,800mAh, up from 2,510mAh in the original. I suppose any improvement is a good thing, but it also means Razr owners are working with a battery that's still significantly smaller than most other phones out there.
  Naturally, Motorola contends that battery life shouldn't be an issue. That's partially because of what it learned about how people use their Razrs --customers apparently really enjoy using the phone's 2.7-inch external touch screen, which draws much less power than the big internal display. The problem is, the original Razr's software didn't allow for too much flexibility; you could use that smaller screen to check your notifications, frame up a selfie, or fire off a canned response to a message, but that was about it.
 The biggest change Motorola made for this second-generation Razr is that those limitations have been lifted. Swipe right on that small screen, and you'll get quick access to a handful of apps Motorola has curated for their ease of use, like YouTube, Google News, Google Home, and more. Even better, you can add  whatever app you want to that list of shortcuts, so there's a decent chance you can get through at least part of your day without having to open the Razr at all.
   By now, it might sound like Motorola has improved this new Razr on all fronts, and that's very nearly true. There are only a few things Motorola  didn't change here, like its 6.2-inch flexible internal display. It's the exact same panel they used last time, and while that's not necessarily a bad thing, I was still hoping a second-gen Razr screen would run at a resolution higher than 876 x 2,142.
 Maybe more curious is the fact that, in the United States anyway, Motorola just plans to call this phone the "Razr," and doesn't plan to differentiate it from the Verizon-only model it released earlier this year. I guess that makes sense on some level -- people walking into an AT&T or T-Mobile store probably weren't expecting to get the same device they find at Verizon, but lumping these two phones into the same brand bucket seems very strange when you consider just how different they are.
 Then again, the foldable landscape as a whole is pretty strange right now -- Motorola's in good company. If the company's refined approach to the Razr is calling out to you, you won't have to wait very long: It'll be available unlocked this fall at Best Buy, B&H Photo, Amazon.com, and Motorola.com, plus through AT&T and T-Mobile around the same time.
title: 摩托罗拉的5G Razr几乎在所有方面都比第一代更好
title_en: Motorola's 5G Razr Is Better Than the Original In Almost Every Way

note:
GameStop的最新季度收益报告显示，这家长期陷入困境的游戏零售商并没有太多扭亏为盈的迹象。4月至6月这个季度的销售额同比下降了26.7%。即使考虑到永久门店关闭和与COVID相关的营业时间减少，所谓的可比同店销售额仍同比下降12.7%。消息传出后，GameStop已经低迷的股票下跌了近8%。
GameStop仍然公开认为，在即将发布的新索尼和微软游戏机上有机会获利，这可能会在短期内帮助其业务扭亏为盈。但我们有理由相信，由于游戏机选项完全去掉了光盘驱动器，下一代游戏机实际上可能会让GameStop的长期前景变得更糟。
在财报电话会议上，GameStop首席执行官乔治·谢尔曼承认，数字下载量一直在增长，并表示GameStop并不是在争论数字游戏的增长。但他也试图为GameStop的实体游戏销售进入下一代游戏机的命运带来积极的影响。
谢尔曼说，首先，新的游戏机有一个光盘驱动器。因此，在接下来的七年里，游戏机将同时播放我们销售的物理和数字软件。
然而，这只是部分正确的。Xbox Series S和PlayStation5 Digital Edition实际上都不会有光盘驱动器。虽然GameStop确实销售一些数字软件，但其大部分业务来自销售新的实体游戏和高利润率的二手游戏。
当然，我们不知道有多大比例的消费者会选择这些无光盘游戏机。GameStop 2013年的一项调查显示，大约60%的消费者甚至不会考虑不玩二手游戏的游戏机。在过去的七年里，人们的态度可能发生了变化，但很难知道到底有多大。
尽管如此，较低的硬件成本肯定会使无盘游戏机成为这一代许多潜在客户的一个有吸引力的选择。Xbox Series S比功能更强大、配备磁盘驱动器的Series X便宜200美元，这笔钱可以用来购买大量的数字游戏。
无论如何，每一位在下一代放弃光驱的游戏机玩家都是消费者，未来对GameStop这样的零售商的需求将会大大减少。虽然其中一些玩家可能已经忽略了光盘上的游戏，但现在他们至少可以选择购买光盘上的一些游戏(特别是如果有特别吸引人的收藏品可供选择的话)。
从11月份开始，游戏机市场的很大一部分人甚至不能考虑GameStop的面包和黄油光盘销售。
谢尔曼在财报电话会议上证实，GameStop将在其门店销售这些无光盘驱动器的游戏机，此举类似于Tower Records在其实体专辑销售陷入困境时决定销售iPod的世界。但谢尔曼也试图保持一种健康的怀疑态度，即下一代游戏机购买者是否会想要放弃他们的光盘驱动器。
他说，消费者喜欢游戏的物理方面。他们收集它们，然后以旧换新的方式增加价值。因此，随着软件以显著更好的图形继续发展，它不会占用宝贵的存储空间，没有宽带互联网的人也可以买到光盘。
肯定有一部分市场继续看重实体游戏，谢尔曼说得对，在可预见的未来，实体游戏的生命将继续存在。然而，与此同时，这个市场的规模已经在缩小，尽管家用游戏机上随处可见光盘驱动器。
索尼表示，在截至6月份的季度里，其全部游戏销售额的74%来自数字购买。即使这个数字暂时被冠状病毒相关的在家订单夸大了，索尼去年报告的53%的数字完整游戏销售率也没有。
不难找到其他证据表明，纯数字游戏的支出在上升，而实体光盘的支出在下降。虽然手机游戏的兴起与此有一定关系，但游戏机市场也不能幸免于同样的趋势，而不是光盘。
谢尔曼试图在这一势不可挡的趋势面前摆出一副勇敢的姿态，他辩称，游戏下载并不像人们所说的那样。他说，在美国和世界的大多数地区，宽带税仍然在增加。随着在家工作活动的显著增加，许多人被迫在宽带使用上做出权衡，这是物理游戏的天然优势。
他补充说，凭借我们目前的能力和增加的当天送货功能，我们相信大多数消费者可以比下载更快地获得实体游戏副本。
在我们听来，这些听起来像是纸质杂志出版商在面对互联网新闻消费上升时提出的那种论点，或者是百视达视频作为流媒体视频的崛起。在美国，随着平均下载速度超过游戏文件大小的增长，以及数字游戏订阅服务提供越来越好的价值，购买光盘上的游戏对越来越大的细分市场可能看起来越来越不具吸引力。
也许GameStop将能够转向手机售货亭模式，从一个集中的物理聚集场所销售和维修游戏硬件和配件(以及与游戏相关的收藏品)。该公司已经概述了一项雄心勃勃的计划，即在每家GameStop门店内转型为一个社交和文化游戏中心。但任何未来依赖其目前主要通过光盘销售游戏的模式，似乎都不是一个好的长期押注。
GameStop很可能无论如何都是注定要失败的，但Gamestop肯定会破坏实体媒体的价值，从而导致他们自己的厄运。以比新版低3美元的价格出售二手游戏，以8美元的价格购买二手游戏，肯定会打折物理媒体的理论价值。这在短期内无疑对利润有利，但Gamestop的存在在很大程度上依赖于他们毒害的转售循环。由于客户认为实体媒体的价值较低，相比之下，数字下载变得更具吸引力。随着数字下载的其他优势变得越来越普遍，在许多消费者看来，数字下载的其他优势抵消了成本的边际增长。他们永远无法撤销这一点。TL/DR：你可以欺骗你的客户……。但他们可能不会回来了。
长期以来，我一直在想，为什么GameStop和其他零售商不运营数字下载亭，这样你就可以连接硬盘，加载一系列游戏，然后把它带回家，连接到你的游戏机上，然后购买它们玩。
我不确定1)会不会有巨大的市场(为什么不直接把游戏下载到家里，省去一趟旅行呢？)。2)游戏机制造商会合作(既然你可以直接在游戏机上销售下载内容，为什么要引入中间商来抽成呢)。
我以前想过这个问题，我得到的两个答案是：1)我认为它将主要吸引农村地区，或者任何其他互联网速度很慢和/或有数据上限的地方。信息亭的优势是能够缓存数十到数百个游戏。它可能会受益于坐落在一个互联网比周边更好的城市/城镇，可以在夜间将下载卸载到非高峰时间，更糟糕的是，Gamestop甚至可以直接将硬盘装入自助服务亭，安装新游戏。2)Gamestop可以将此作为购买店内数字代码或购买物理光盘的好处，在这种情况下，下载将只是游戏的更新/补丁。也就是说，一旦你把它放到售货亭里，你真的需要把它放在Gamestop里吗？也许Redbox可以把这个加到他们的售货亭里？尽管Redbox最近退出了游戏租赁业务，因此这可能不是一个巨大的市场。Https://www.theverge.com/2019/12/9/2100..。S-Business
note_en:
The latest quarterly earnings report from GameStop doesn't show much sign of a turnaround for the  long-troubled game  retailer. Sales were down 26.7 percent year over year for the April through June quarter. Even accounting for  permanent store closures and  COVID-related reduced operating hours, so-called comparable "same-store" sales were still down 12.7 percent year over year. GameStop's already depressed stock is down nearly 8 percent on the news, as of this writing.
 GameStop still publicly sees an "opportunity to capitalize" on the upcoming release of new Sony and Microsoft consoles, which could help turn its business around in the short term. But there's some reason to believe the coming generation of consoles could actually make GameStop's long-term prospects worse, thanks to console options that get rid of disc drives entirely.
  In  an earnings call, GameStop CEO George Sherman acknowledged that "there has been growth in digitally downloaded games" and said GameStop is "not debating the growth of digital gaming." But he also tried to put a positive spin on the fate of GameStop's physical game sales going into the next generation of consoles.
 "First, new consoles have a disc drive," Sherman said. "So for the next seven years, the consoles will play both the physical and digital software that we sell."
 That's only partially true, though. Both the  Xbox Series S and the  PlayStation 5 Digital Edition actually  won't have a disc drive. And while GameStop does sell some digital software, the bulk of its business comes from the sale of new physical games and  high-margin pre-owned games.
 We don't know what percentage of consumers will opt for these disc-free consoles, of course. A GameStop survey from 2013 suggested that  60 percent of consumers wouldn't even consider a console that doesn't play used games. Attitudes may have changed in the last seven years, but it's hard to know just how much.
 Still, lower hardware costs could definitely make discless consoles an attractive option to many potential customers this generation. The Xbox Series S  is $200 cheaper than the more-powerful, disc-drive-equipped Series X, a sum that could go toward a lot of digital game purchases.
  Regardless, every console gamer that gives up a disc drive in the next generation is a consumer that is going to have much less need for a retailer like GameStop going forward. And while some of those players might already be ignoring games on disc, right now they at least have the  option of buying  some titles on disc (especially if there's  a particularly attractive collector's edition available).
 Starting in November, a significant chunk of the console market won't even be able to consider GameStop's bread-and-butter disc sales.
  Sherman confirmed in the earnings call that GameStop will sell these disc-drive-free consoles in its stores, a move akin to a world where Tower Records decided to sell iPods as its physical album sales cratered. But Sherman also tried to maintain a healthy skepticism that next-generation console buyers would want to give up their disc drives.
 "Consumers like the physical aspects of games," he said. "They collect them and they add value as a trade-in. So as software continues to evolve with dramatically better graphics, it does not take up valuable storage space and discs are available to those without broadband Internet."
 There is definitely a segment of the market that  continues to value physical games, and Sherman is right that "the life of physical game is here to stay for the foreseeable future." At the same time, though, the size of that market is already shrinking, even as disc drives are ubiquitous on home consoles.
 In the quarter ending in June, Sony said that  a full 74 percent of its full game sales came as digital purchases. Even if that number is temporarily inflated by coronavirus-related stay-at-home orders, the 53-percent digital full game sales ratio Sony reported last year was not.
 It's not hard to find  other  evidence that spending on purely digital games is rising while spending on physical discs is falling. And while the rise of mobile gaming has something to do with that, the console market  isn't immune from  the same trend away from discs.
 Sherman tried to put up a brave front against this overwhelming trend, arguing that game downloads aren't all they're cracked up to be. "Across most regions of the US and the world, there remains an increased taxation on broadband," he said. "With significant increases in work-from-home activities, many are forced to make trade-offs on broadband usage, a natural advantage for physical gaming.
 "With our current capabilities and added same-day delivery, we believe most consumers can get a physical game copy faster than it takes to download," he added.
 To our ear, these sound like the kinds of arguments  print magazine publishers made in the face of rising Internet news consumption, or that  Blockbuster video made as streaming video rose in prominence. With  average download speeds outpacing game file size increases in the US, and  digital game subscription services providing better and better value, buying a game on a disc is likely going to look less and less appealing to larger and larger segments of the market.
 Maybe GameStop will be able to pivot to a cell phone kiosk model, selling and servicing game hardware and accessories (alongside game-related collectibles) from a centralized physical gathering place. The company has  already outlined an ambitious plan to transform into "a social and cultural hub of gaming within each GameStop store." But any future GameStop that relies on its current model of primarily selling games on discs doesn't seem like a good long-term bet.
  Gamestop is likely doomed regardless but Gamestop certain contributed to their own doom by undermining the value of physical media. Selling used games for $3 less than new copies and offering $8 for buying a used game certainly discounted the theoretical value in physical media.  It was great for profits in the short term no doubt but Gamestop's existence was largely dependent on that resale loop that they poisoned. As customers saw less value in physical media digital downloads in comparison became more attractive. As it became more common that the other advantages of digital downloads offset the marginal increase in cost in the eyes of many consumers. They will never be able to undo that.  TL/DR: You can screw over your customers ... but they might not come back.
  I have long wondered why GameStop and other retailers don't operate "digital download kiosks" that would allow you to connected a hard drive, load it with a bunch of games, then take it home, connect it to your console and buy them to play.
  I'm not sure that 1) there would be a huge market for that (why not just download the games directly at home and save a trip?) and 2) that the console makers would play ball (why introduce a middleman to take a cut when you can just sell downloads directly on the console itself).
 I've thought of this before, and the two answers I have are:  1) I think it'd appeal to mainly rural areas, or anywhere else with slow internet and/or data caps. The kiosk would have the advantage of being able to cache dozens to hundreds of games. It might benefit from being located in a city/town with better internet than around it, could offload downloads to "off-peak" overnight times, and worse comes to worse Gamestop could even just ship HDDs to insert into the kiosk with new games.  2) Gamestop could offer this as a benefit for buying a digital code in-store, or for buying a physical disc, in which case the download would just be the updates/patches for the game.  That said, once you put it in a kiosk, do you really need it to be in a Gamestop? Could maybe RedBox add this to their kiosks? Although RedBox recently got out of renting games, so this might not be a huge market.  https://www.theverge.com/2019/12/9/2100 ... s-business
title: 没有光驱的游戏机选项可能是GameStop的最后死亡之地
title_en: Console Options Without Disc Drives Could Be GameStop's Final Death Knell

航天局已经预订了商业着陆器上前往月球表面的机器人乘车，并计划对载人任务采取同样的做法，现在的目标是向私人公司支付收集月球污垢的费用。
美国宇航局局长吉姆·布里登斯廷今天(9月30日)说，底线是，我们将重新购买一些月球土壤，以证明这是可以做到的。10)在安全世界基金会空间可持续发展峰会的在线演讲中。
新闻：@NASA正在从一家商业供应商那里购买月球土壤！现在是建立开采和交易空间资源的监管确定性的时候了。更多信息：https://t.co/B1F5bS6pEy pic.twitter.com/oWu GHnB8ev 2020年9月10日。
布里登斯汀在今天的一篇博客文章中解释说，该机构刚刚发布了一份征求建议书(RFP)，大意是这样的。NASA希望来自美国或海外的私营公司在2024年之前获得1.8盎司至18盎司(50至500克)的月球材料，并正式将这些材料的所有权转让给月球表面的航天局。
NASA将为每个这些缓存支付15,000至25,000美元，其中80%的资金将在样本采集后交付。布里登斯廷补充说，公司在签署合同时将获得10%，在发射航天器后将获得10%。
如果一切按计划进行，NASA最终将把月球物质带回地球。(当然，航天局在这里已经有相当多的月球岩石了。阿波罗宇航员在1969年至1972年间带回了842磅(约合382公斤)的月球材料。)。
布里登斯廷说，你可以在这里找到的新的RFP的主要目标是刺激和正常化月球资源的开采和销售。例如，参与的公司可能会选择收集远远超过18盎司的材料，并将多余的出售给非NASA买家。
布里登斯廷在今天的演讲中说：现在，我们正在努力证明资源可以被开采，也可以被交易这一概念。而且不仅在公司或个人之间进行交易，而且还可以在国家之间和跨国界-其他国家的私人个人之间进行交易。
他补充说，NASA计划在未来通过RFP的其他迭代继续鼓励这样的项目。布里登斯廷强调，所有这些活动都将根据1967年的“外层空间条约”进行，该条约是国际空间法的支柱。
月球资源的利用对于在月球上和月球周围建立可持续的人类存在至关重要，NASA的目标是通过其Artemis载人探测计划在本世纪20年代末实现这一目标。NASA官员表示，这项月球工作将帮助该机构为本世纪30年代的载人火星之旅做准备。

note_en:
The space agency, which has already   booked robotic rides to the lunar surface on commercial landers and plans to   do the same with crewed missions, now aims to pay private companies to collect moon dirt.
  "The bottom line is, we're gonna buy some lunar soil for the purpose of demonstrating that it can be done," NASA Administrator Jim Bridenstine said today (Sept. 10) during an online presentation at the Secure World Foundation's Summit for Space Sustainability.
   NEWS: @NASA is buying lunar soil from a commercial provider! It’s time to establish the regulatory certainty to extract and trade space resources. More: https://t.co/B1F5bS6pEy pic.twitter.com/oWuGHnB8ev September 10, 2020
 The agency just issued a request for proposals (RFP) to this effect, Bridenstine explained in a   blog post today. NASA wants private companies, from the United States or abroad, to snag 1.8 ounces to 18 ounces (50 to 500 grams) of lunar material by 2024 and officially transfer ownership of the stuff to the space agency on the lunar surface.
 NASA will pay $15,000 to $25,000 for each of these caches, with 80% of the money delivered after sample collection. Companies will get 10% upon signing a contract and 10% after launching their spacecraft, Bridenstine added.
 NASA will eventually bring the lunar material down to Earth, if all goes according to plan. (The space agency already has a considerable stash of moon rocks here, of course. The Apollo astronauts brought home 842 lbs., or 382 kilograms, of   lunar material between 1969 and 1972.)
 The main goal of the new RFP, which you can find   here, is to stimulate and normalize the extraction and sale of lunar resources, Bridenstine said. For example, participating companies may choose to collect far more than 18 ounces of material and sell the excess to non-NASA buyers.
 "Right now, we're trying to prove the concept that resources can be extracted, and they can be traded," Bridenstine said in today's presentation. "And not just traded among companies or private individuals, but also among countries and across borders — private individuals in other countries."
  NASA plans to continue encouraging such projects with "other iterations" of the RFP in the future, he added. All such activities, Bridenstine stressed, will be conducted in accordance with the 1967   Outer Space Treaty, which is the backbone of international space law.
 The use of lunar resources is critical to establishing a sustainable human presence on and around the moon, a goal NASA aims to achieve by the late 2020s via its   Artemis program of crewed exploration. This lunar work will help the agency prepare for crewed trips to Mars in the 2030s, NASA officials have said.
 Mike Wall is the author of "Out There" (Grand Central Publishing, 2018; illustrated by Karl Tate), a book about the search for alien life. Follow him on Twitter @michaeldwall. Follow us on Twitter @Spacedotcom or Facebook.
